# UC-14.3.48 · Pressure & Humidity Sensor Correlation for Leakage Detection

## Description

Detects water leaks or condensation damage early by correlating pressure drop with humidity rise in sealed enclosures.

## Value

Detects water leaks or condensation damage early by correlating pressure drop with humidity rise in sealed enclosures.

## Implementation

Deploy Edge Hub in sealed enclosure (electrical room, equipment cabinet) with optional pressure sensor (±0.12 hPa accuracy) and built-in humidity sensor exposed to enclosure air. Configure 5-minute polling. Monitor pressure trend: sealed enclosure pressure should remain stable (±1 hPa). Pressure drop + humidity rise = leakage from outside or failed seal. Humidity rise alone = internal moisture generation (faulty equipment). Implement baseline: first week = normal enclosure profile. Alert if pressure drops >1 hPa/hour (rapid leak). Store local SQLite data for 30+ days to track seasonal humidity variations. Trigger maintenance ticket on leak detection.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (pressure + humidity sensors), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=pressure OR metric_name=humidity, `sourcetype=edge_hub`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy Edge Hub in sealed enclosure (electrical room, equipment cabinet) with optional pressure sensor (±0.12 hPa accuracy) and built-in humidity sensor exposed to enclosure air. Configure 5-minute polling. Monitor pressure trend: sealed enclosure pressure should remain stable (±1 hPa). Pressure drop + humidity rise = leakage from outside or failed seal. Humidity rise alone = internal moisture generation (faulty equipment). Implement baseline: first week = normal enclosure profile. Alert if pres…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats avg(pressure) as press, avg(humidity) as rh by host, _time span=5m
| delta press as press_change
| stats avg(press_change) as avg_press_delta, stdev(rh) as rh_volatility by host
| eval leak_risk=case(
    avg_press_delta < -0.5 AND rh_volatility > 10, "CRITICAL_LEAK",
    avg_press_delta < -0.2 AND rh_volatility > 5, "POTENTIAL_LEAK",
    1=1, "NORMAL")
| where leak_risk!="NORMAL"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Pressure & Humidity Sensor Correlation for Leakage Detection** — Detects water leaks or condensation damage early by correlating pressure drop with humidity rise in sealed enclosures.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=pressure OR metric_name=humidity, `sourcetype=edge_hub`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (pressure + humidity sensors), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
• Pipeline stage (see **Pressure & Humidity Sensor Correlation for Leakage Detection**): delta press as press_change
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **leak_risk** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where leak_risk!="NORMAL"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pressure vs humidity scatter plot, leak risk gauge, enclosure seal integrity trend.

## SPL

```spl
| mstats avg(pressure) as press, avg(humidity) as rh by host, _time span=5m
| delta press as press_change
| stats avg(press_change) as avg_press_delta, stdev(rh) as rh_volatility by host
| eval leak_risk=case(
    avg_press_delta < -0.5 AND rh_volatility > 10, "CRITICAL_LEAK",
    avg_press_delta < -0.2 AND rh_volatility > 5, "POTENTIAL_LEAK",
    1=1, "NORMAL")
| where leak_risk!="NORMAL"
```

## Visualization

Pressure vs humidity scatter plot, leak risk gauge, enclosure seal integrity trend.
